The Game for your browser. Turn the internet into your gameboard, see browsing stats, and compete on the global high scores.

You've visited thousands of websites. What do you have to show for it?

Cheevos turns your daily browsing into a global competition. Every page you visit, file you download, and bookmark you save earns XP and unlocks achievements, building a rank that reflects how you actually use the internet.

Cheevos players have opened over 20,000 tabs and scrolled a combined 19 miles through the web. Every session adds to that.

Earn XP for what you already do
Every website visit, download, and bookmark counts. Watch your rank climb from Iron to Platinum as your real browsing habits are rewarded.

Unlock 100+ achievements across 10 categories
Navigation, Productivity, Time, Media, and more. Some achievements are obvious. Others are hidden: discover secret browser pages, rare milestones, and easter eggs most users never find.

Compete on global leaderboards
See where you rank against players worldwide. Live high scores update in real-time. Can you reach Diamond tier?

Browse with friends
Form a Party of up to 10 players, earn group XP, and compete in team challenges. Your squad's combined browsing builds a shared rank.

Works across Firefox and Chrome
Your XP, rank, and achievements sync across both browsers. Switch devices without losing your progress.

---
Privacy by Design — not just a tagline

All achievement logic runs entirely inside your browser. We never see your URLs, your history, or what you're reading. The only things that ever reach our servers are your XP total, your level, and your unlocked badge IDs — the minimum needed to power the leaderboard. Your raw browsing data stays on your machine, always.

You can delete your account and all synced data at any time from your dashboard.

---
Stop boring browsing. Install Cheevos and start playing the web.

---
Permissions — why we need them

We only request permissions that directly enable a specific feature. Here's exactly what each one does:

Browsing history and website activity
Required to detect achievement-triggering destinations — things like reaching a Wikipedia article, finding a hidden browser page, or completing a navigation milestone. We never store or sync your URLs. Instead, the extension creates a truncated one-way SHA-256 hash of each domain locally on your machine. The raw address is discarded immediately.

Bookmarks
Required for achievements tied to your curation habits — how many bookmarks you've saved, how long you've kept them, and how you organize them. Your folder names and URLs never leave your device.

Downloads
Required for achievements like Data Hoarder and Big Catch, which track cumulative download sizes and file counts. Filenames and URLs are processed locally and never synced.

Notifications
Used to announce achievement unlocks and major XP milestones in real-time. You can disable these in your browser's notification settings at any time.

---
That's it. No ad tracking, no behavioral profiling, no third-party data sharing. Just a game built on top of your existing browsing habits.

_THIS EXTENSION IS IN ALPHA_ Superkeys allow users to add short keys for websites and make search query in those sites.

Superkeys is a browser add-on/extension which gives users superpower for searching. It's like the mac's glorious SPOTLIGHT app but for browsers.

Features:

    🗝️ Keys: Customizable keywords for quick search
    🔍 Query URL: Each key has it's query url which can be useful to perform direct search on websites.
    🪺 Sub-keys: Each key can have multiple sub Keys. eg: tw new : https://twitter.com/compose/tweet
    This sub-key is used to create new twitter post
    🌶️ Context Menu: All sub-keys with query Url enabled, will add a context menu to search on that site

Quickly fill in links from browser history.

How to use:
1. To open the search window, you can:
    a. Right-click on the page and select "Search Links"
    b. Use the keyboard shortcut (defaults to `Ctrl+Shift+K`)
2. Start typing to search through history.
3. Use keyboard arrows to highlight the result you want.
4. Once you have the result selected, you can:
    a. Press `Enter` to insert the link into the text box (will copy to clipboard if nothing is selected)
    b. Click the clipboard icon to copy the link.
    c. Click the arrow icon to open the link in a new tab.
Lightning-fast universal launcher. Press ⌘. to instantly search tabs, bookmarks, history, or the web.

Focus Dock: The Ultimate Universal Search & Productivity Launcher

Stop digging through hundreds of tabs. Focus Dock is the fastest Universal Search and Tab Manager for Chrome. Press ⌘+. (Cmd+Period) from any webpage to instantly launch your command center. No signup, no configuration—just pure productivity.

Why Focus Dock is the #1 Productivity Launcher: Our "Zero-Setup" architecture means you are productive the second you install. Whether you need a bookmark manager, a tab search tool, or a quick notes app, Focus Dock handles it all through a single, keyboard-first interface.

🚀 Core Features (100% Free): 

• Instant Universal Search: Find open tabs, bookmarks, and browser history with lightning-fast fuzzy matching (even with typos). 
• Tab Management & Groups: Use /groups to organize your workspace or /autogroup to sort tabs by domain instantly. 
• Quick Notes: Type /note to capture ideas without leaving your current page. • Reading List: Save any article with ⌘+Shift+S and access it later with /read. 
• Workspaces: Save and restore entire tab sessions for different projects using /save. 
• Clipboard History: Access your recently copied links and text using /clip.

⚡ Smart Commands for Power Users: Focus Dock turns your browser into a command line. 
Navigate like a pro with 100% keyboard support: 

• /github — Instantly see all open GitHub tabs. 
• /workspaces — Switch between project sessions. 
• /index — Rebuild your local search index for real-time results.

🔒 Privacy-First Design: 

• Everything Stays Local: Your history, notes, and search data never leave your browser. 
• No Accounts Required: Start using all core features immediately without a login. 
• No Tracking: We believe your browsing data belongs to you.

Free vs. Pro (Flagship Access): Focus Dock is free to use forever with all core features included. Upgrade to Pro for unlimited workspaces, unlimited notes, 100 clipboard history items, and visual tab thumbnails.

Perfect for: 

• Developers managing complex documentation and dozens of tabs. 
• Researchers building a Second Brain and gathering resources. 
• Students switching between multiple project workspaces. 
• Power Users who want to work at the speed of thought.

Stop wasting time switching tabs. Experience the power of a universal browser launcher. Install Focus Dock and press ⌘+. now.

Multi-monitor DICOM display for Enterprise PACS with preserved layouts and intelligent protocol matching.

LUECI ViewAssist                                                                                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                                                                                              
  LUECI ViewAssist is a productivity companion for radiologists, technologists,                                                                                                               
  and PACS administrators working in Enterprise PACS / DICOM viewer                                                                                                                           
  environments. It speeds up routine reading by managing hanging protocols,                                                                                                                   
  preserving multi-monitor layouts, and aligning the right protocol with each                                                                                                                 
  study as it opens.                                                                                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                                                                                              
  WHAT IT DOES                                                                                                                                                                                
                                                                                                                                                                                              
  • Multi-Monitor Awareness
    Detects how many displays the PACS viewer is opened on and adapts
    protocol suggestions to single-monitor, dual-monitor, and three-or-more                                                                                                                   
    monitor configurations. No more re-arranging windows after every study.                                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                                                                                              
  • Hanging Protocol Library                                                                                                                                                                  
    Ships with a curated, editable library of hanging protocols covering
    common modalities and body parts (CR, CT, MR, US, XA, MG, NM, PT and                                                                                                                      
    more). Protocols are stored locally in your browser — nothing is sent to                                                                                                                  
    an external server.
                                                                                                                                                                                              
  • Layout Capture
    One-click capture of the current viewer layout (series placement, monitor
    assignment, image counts, prior comparison setup) so it can be re-applied                                                                                                                 
    to the next matching study.
                                                                                                                                                                                              
  • Study-Aware Protocol Matching
    Reads study metadata exposed by the viewer — modality, study description,
    DICOM Body Part Examined (0018,0015), image count, and presence of                                                                                                                        
    priors — and ranks the closest protocols from your library. Vendor-
    specific body part codes from common modalities (FUJIFILM, Carestream,                                                                                                                    
    GE, etc.) are normalized so matches stay consistent across equipment.
                                                                                                                                                                                              
  • Prior Study Handling
    Recognizes when prior studies are loaded alongside the current exam and
    prefers comparison-friendly layouts when priors are present.                                                                                                                              
  
  • Worksheet & Report Templates                                                                                                                                                              
    Optional worksheet capture and report-template library to keep dictation
    structure consistent between similar studies.                                                                                                                                             
  
  • Daily Usage Stats                                                                                                                                                                         
    Lightweight in-popup counter that shows how many protocols and reports
    you have applied today. Resets daily, stored locally.
                                                                                                                                                                                              
  WHO IT IS FOR
                                                                                                                                                                                              
  • Diagnostic radiologists who read across multiple modalities and want
    consistent, reproducible viewer layouts.
  • Technologists and lead techs maintaining standardized hanging protocols                                                                                                                   
    across a department.
  • PACS administrators rolling out a uniform reading-room configuration.                                                                                                                     
  • Reading-room super-users who want their preferred layout to follow them
    between workstations.                                                                                                                                                                     
  
  HOW IT WORKS                                                                                                                                                                                
                  
  1. Install the extension and open your Enterprise PACS viewer in the
     browser.
  2. ViewAssist reads non-PHI study attributes (modality, study description,
     body part, image counts, monitor count) from the active viewer tab.                                                                                                                      
  3. The extension scores its local protocol library against those
     attributes and surfaces the best match plus alternatives.                                                                                                                                
  4. Apply the protocol with one click, or capture the current layout to
     save your own.                                                                                                                                                                           
                  
  PRIVACY & DATA HANDLING                                                                                                                                                                     
  
  • All processing is performed locally in your browser. No study data,                                                                                                                       
    patient data, or images are uploaded to any external service by this
    extension.
  • Protocols, report templates, captured layouts, and usage counters are
    stored in your browser's local IndexedDB and chrome.storage. They never                                                                                                                   
    leave the device unless you explicitly export them.
  • The extension does not contain analytics, tracking pixels, advertising                                                                                                                    
    SDKs, or third-party telemetry.                                                                                                                                                           
  • No account, login, or registration is required.
                                                                                                                                                                                              
  COMPATIBILITY   

  • Designed for browser-based Enterprise PACS / DICOM viewers running over                                                                                                                   
    HTTP or HTTPS in Chromium-based browsers.
  • Works on single-monitor laptops, dual-monitor reading stations, and                                                                                                                       
    three-or-more monitor diagnostic workstations.
  • Manifest V3, service-worker based — no legacy background pages.                                                                                                                           
  
  DISCLAIMER                                                                                                                                                                                  
                  
  LUECI ViewAssist is a workflow and layout productivity tool. It does not                                                                                                                    
  make, support, or replace clinical decisions, diagnoses, or
  interpretations. It does not modify, store, or transmit DICOM image data.                                                                                                                   
  Always follow your institution's PACS, privacy, and clinical governance
  policies before installing extensions on a reading workstation.

Sidebar extension for managing tabs in a tree structure

もう、タブの迷子にならない。
Chromeのサイドパネルを利用して、増えすぎたタブを「ツリー形式」や「最近使った順」でスマートに整理・管理できる究極のタブマネージャーです。

【魅力的な詳細説明】
「調べ物をしていたら、いつの間にかタブが細くなって中身が分からない…」
「さっきまで見ていたタブがどこにあるか見つからない」
そんなストレスを、Tree Tab Managerが解消します。

Google Chromeの最新機能（Side Panel API / Manifest V3）をフル活用し、ブラウジングを妨げない快適な操作感を実現しました。

【主な特徴】
🌿 視覚的に整理する「ツリー表示」
リンクから開いたタブを自動的に「親子関係」として階層表示。情報の関連性が一目でわかります。ドラッグ＆ドロップで親子関係を自由に入れ替えることも可能です。

🕐 迷わず戻れる「最近開いた順モード」
ツリー構造だけでなく、最後に使った時刻順にタブを並べ替えることができます。たくさんのタブを開きっぱなしにしていても、今必要なタブにすぐアクセスできます。

📂 完璧な「グループ機能」連携
Chrome標準のタブグループに完全対応。サイドパネルからグループの色変更や名前の編集、一括操作がスムーズに行えます。

📌 固定タブ（ピン）専用セクション
大事なタブは上部に固定。通常のタブと切り分けて表示されるため、常に使いたいツールを見失いません。

💾 安心の「セッション保存」
現在のタブの状態（親子関係やピン留め、グループ状況まで！）をまるごと保存。後から別のウィンドウで一気に復元できるので、作業の切り替えも自由自在です。

🔍 履歴・ブックマークもこれひとつで
タブ管理だけでなく、履歴やブックマークの閲覧・検索機能も搭載。サイドパネルを開くだけで、ブラウザ内のあらゆる情報にアクセス可能です。

【その他のこだわり機能】
3つの表示モード: 通常・コンパクト・ミニから、あなたの画面サイズに最適なデザインを選べます。

タブ名のカスタム: わかりにくいページタイトルを自分好みの名前に書き換え可能。

ドメインまとめ: 同じドメインのタブをワンクリックで子タブとして集約。

ダークモード対応: 目に優しいデザインで、長時間の作業をサポート。

【使い方は簡単】
拡張機能をインストール。

ツールバーの拡張機能アイコンをクリックしてサイドパネルを開く。

左側のタブ一覧から、新しいブラウジング体験をスタート！

Keyboard hint mode: label targets on the page, type the hint to follow links and buttons.

UPDATE: More fonts and customizable font size 

KeySurge turns your keyboard into a supercharged navigation tool. Press the activation shortcut and every clickable target on the page gets a hint label. Type the label to instantly follow that link, click that button, or switch tabs — no mouse needed.

Features
- Instant hint mode — labels every link, button, and interactive element on the page
- Tab Search — press [ in hint mode to fuzzy-search and switch to any open tab
- Bookmark & History pickers — jump to any bookmark or recent page without lifting your hands
- XP Level system — earn XP with every click and progress through tiers: INIT → SCRIPT → CORE → KERNEL → DAEMON → PHANTOM and beyond
 - Live HUD — a compact overlay shows your current level and XP progress to the next tier while hints are open
- Stats dashboard — track total clicks, accuracy, average speed, fastest click, best streak, and 14-day activity
- Shareable stats card — export a PNG of your stats with speed sparkline and daily activity graph
- 17 themes
- Achievements — unlock badges as you hit milestones
- Accessibility-friendly — reduce motion mode, minimal mode, and custom UI fonts supported


All data stays local — no tracking, no remote servers, no account required.

Lightning-fast command palette for Chrome. Search tabs, bookmarks, history instantly. Arc-inspired design with frecency ranking.

Kord is a lightning-fast command palette for Chrome, inspired by Arc Browser and tools like Raycast.

  Press ⌘K (Mac) or Ctrl+K (Windows/Linux) to instantly search everything in your browser — without leaving
   the current tab.

  ────────────────────────
  FEATURES
  ────────────────────────

  🔍 UNIVERSAL SEARCH
  Search across all your open tabs, bookmarks, and browsing history in one place. Results appear in
  milliseconds.

  ⚡ FRECENCY RANKING
  Kord learns your habits. The more you use an item, the higher it ranks — so your most-visited pages
  always appear first.

  🖥️  BROWSER COMMANDS
  Execute common browser actions directly from the palette:
  • New Tab / Duplicate Tab
  • Close Tab / Close Other Tabs
  • Pin / Unpin Tab
  • Mute / Unmute Tab
  • Go Back / Go Forward
  • Reload Tab
  • Clear Cache & Refresh
  • Clear Cookies & Refresh
  • Open Downloads, Extensions, Settings, History
  • Restore Recently Closed Tabs

  🔖 BOOKMARK MANAGEMENT
  Search bookmarks instantly and open them in the current or a new tab.

  🕘 HISTORY SEARCH
  Find any page you've visited before, without opening the history page.

  ────────────────────────
  HOW TO USE
  ────────────────────────

  1. Press ⌘K or Ctrl+K on any page to open Kord
  2. Start typing to search tabs, bookmarks, or history
  3. Press Tab to switch to Command Mode for browser actions
  4. Use ↑↓ arrow keys to navigate, Enter to confirm, Esc to close

  ────────────────────────
  PRIVACY
  ────────────────────────

  Kord processes all data locally within your browser. No data is sent to external servers. Your browsing
  history and bookmarks never leave your device.
הרחבה להגנה על ילדים מפני תוכן לא הולם.

Hawkly - Kids Guardian

שחרור גירסה 2.0 (14.06.24):
• כעת ניתן להוסיף מילים על ידי מרקור שלהם עם סמן העכבר, מקש ימני, ולחיצה על "חסום מילה זו".
**במידה וחסמתם מילה ולא קיבלתם כוכביות בצעו רענן (F5) לדף המבוקש.**
• ניתן להפעיל את ההגנות בפעולה מהירה על ידי לחיצה על CTRL + SHIFT + H - סגירה מבוצעת ידני דרך חלונית התוסף (ומבוצעת באמצעות סיסמת ההורים שנרשמו בתחילת התהליך).
• עבודה על באגים ותקלות שנמצאו בדרך.
• ביצוע שינויים ברמה העיצובית.


Hawkly היא מערכת מתקדמת המיועדת להגן על ילדיכם בעת הגלישה באינטרנט, ולהבטיח שהם גולשים בסביבה בטוחה ומוגנת. עם Hawkly, אתם כהורים יכולים להיות רגועים ולדעת שילדיכם לא ייחשפו לתכנים פוגעניים או לא הולמים ברחבי הרשת.

מה תוסף Hawkly מציע לכם?
חסימת אתרים לא הולמים: התוסף מזהה וחוסם אתרים בעלי תוכן פוגעני, כגון אתרי פורנו, צ'אטים בלתי הולמים ואתרי הימורים.

סינון מילים פוגעניות: Hawkly מסנן מילים בוטות ותכנים פוגעניים בטקסטים באינטרנט, מבטיח שהילדים שלכם לא ייחשפו לשפה לא הולמת.
התראות בזמן אמת: התוסף מספק התראות מיידיות על פעילויות חשודות באינטרנט, ומאפשר לכם להגיב בזמן אמת.

שליטה מרחוק: Hawkly מאפשר לכם לנהל את הגדרות התוסף דרך פלטפורמה אינטרנטית, כך שתוכלו לשנות הגדרות ולפקח על הגלישה מכל מקום.

אבטחה משופרת: התוסף כולל שכבות אבטחה נוספות למניעת מעקפים, מבטיח שההגנה על ילדיכם תהיה אפקטיבית ובלתי ניתנת לעקיפה.

אינטגרציה עם רשתות חברתיות: Hawkly מאפשר לחסום תכנים פוגעניים גם ברשתות חברתיות כמו פייסבוק, טוויטר, אינסטגרם ועוד.

מערכת למידת מכונה: מנגנון למידת המכונה של Hawkly מזהה תכנים לא הולמים בצורה אוטומטית וחכמה, ומשתפר כל הזמן כדי להבטיח את ההגנה הטובה ביותר.
Dedicated-window command shell for tabs, windows, and groups.

BMXt is a UI demonstration for keyboard-centric browser operation on Chrome.
It exists because the author is not satisfied with today’s out-of-the-box browser experience.

It has a terminal-style prompt and log, but rather than being a classic terminal emulator alone, it is designed as a broad command surface for steering the whole browser—so you can keep your hands on the keyboard in a relaxed posture and cut down on how often you reach for tabs and windows.

Command output and typed command history are handled primarily in memory for the UI; only capped fields are persisted in chrome.storage.local. The extension page and service worker are not designed to call fetch() against arbitrary third-party HTTPS URLs (the packaged manifest’s CSP, including connect-src, reinforces this). Optional http(s) site access is requested at runtime only when you run commands that inject scripts into web pages.

◆About the name — a play on Browser Manipulator X Terminal:

- X
  - UX improvement
  - A hope that many different people can use it in crossover ways
  - Exponential growth and open-ended possibility through combining commands
- t
  - The first letter of Terminal
  - The idea of an extension layered onto your browser, carried in the letter t

The road has only just begun, but the author values your voices—so they decided to publish on the Chrome Web Store.
They believe the keyboard in front of you can take you “anywhere,” and will keep stacking features while building BMXt as a tool that stays human-centered.


◆Feedback
- Feedback and wishes from using the extension
https://github.com/UNRsports/bmxt/issues
https://x.com/ny0n150226


◆Disclaimer
BMXt is an experimental / trial implementation. UNRsports (the developer) is not liable for any incidents, loss, or damage arising from your use of the extension. You use BMXt at your own discretion and risk.


◆Version history
0.4.0:
- Fixed bugs in nav mode behavior
- Added translation assist (`translate -on` / `translate -off`)
- Adjusted Tab completion menu behavior
- Fixed welcome page open behavior

0.3.8:
- Show a welcome page on install and when the extension version is upgraded
- Fixed tabs mode filtering so keyboard focus no longer jumps to the list unexpectedly
- Revised mouse interaction: text in the prompt and log can be selected while keeping keyboard-first operation as the default
- Improved in-browser cross-search (`find`)
- Improved Tab completion menu behavior

0.3.5:
- Removed Rust to reduce package size
- Added the `:edit` subcommand in tabs mode for editing tab groups and window names
- Implemented IME-style input assistance while typing commands
- Moved list pickers for all commands to side columns beside the terminal and revised picker interaction (`Esc` returns to the prompt; close a column with `<cmd> -exit -list`)
- DOM list picker: focus rows with ↑/↓ and scroll the target page to the matching element
- Added nav mode: virtual cursor overlay on the active tab (`nav -enter`; Alt toggles ON/OFF; arrows move; Enter click/type; Ctrl context menu for text selection, image save, and reload)


0.2.1:
- Added the `dom` command so you can inspect the page source.
- Added the `find` command to search across internal data such as history, bookmarks, and open tabs.

0.1.1:
- Added the split command; add panes with split and remove them with exit (closes the window when the last pane exits)
- With multiple panes from split, Ctrl+Arrow moves focus between panes
- Clarified command behavior rules
- Added an assist menu while typing commands (type "tabs" and press Enter to try it)
- In tabs mode, "/" search: type a phrase and press Enter to highlight matches; press n to jump between them

0.0.9:
- Tab name search and highlight reset in tabs mode now follow Vim-style behavior (clear highlighted matches with :nohlsearch)
- Improved how release notes are specified and shown

◆Commands

| Command | Description |
|----------|------|
| `help` / `?` | Show help |
| `clear` | Clear logs |
| `exit` | Close the BMXt window and clear the session log |
| `split` | Show `-col` / `-row` options; Enter alone restores the prompt to `split ` (with a trailing space); `split -col` adds a pane beside the current one; `split -row` adds a pane below; with multiple panes, Ctrl+Arrow moves focus between panes |
| `tabs` | Show available options, then restore the prompt to `tabs ` (with a trailing space) for continued input |
| `tabs -list [-u]` | Open the tab picker; supports search, multi-select marker `#`, and bulk modes; `/` searches tab titles, Enter highlights matches, `n` jumps between them |
| `tabs -moveurl <url>` | Focus a tab with the given URL or open a new tab (http/https) |
| `tabs -nowurl` | Show the current tab’s URL |
| `dom` | Print available options; Enter alone restores the prompt to `dom ` (trailing space) for the next token |
| `dom -list [--html|--react] [<pattern>]` | Open a read-only DOM picker for the active tab (same picker chrome as `find -list`); flavor `--html` (default) or `--react`; optional `<pattern>` filters rendered lines with a case-insensitive substring (not a regex). ↑/↓ focus scrolls the target tab to the highlighted element. Scriptable http(s) pages only; may prompt for optional site access |
| `nav` | Print usage; Enter alone restores the prompt to `nav ` (trailing space) for `-enter` or `-exit` |
| `nav -enter` | Arm nav mode; **Alt** toggles the virtual cursor overlay on the target tab; arrows move; **Enter** click/type; **Ctrl** opens a context menu (text selection, save image, reload, history) |
| `nav -exit` | Fully disarm nav (turn the overlay **OFF** with **Alt** first) |
| `find` | Print available options; Enter alone restores the prompt to `find ` for a subcommand or flag |
| `find -list [--none|--history|--bookmark|--page] <pattern>` | Open a cross-search picker; default `--none` searches browsing history, bookmarks, and visible text in non-discarded http(s) tabs together; use one flag to limit scope. Case-insensitive substring match (no regex in v1) |
| `find --none <pattern>` | Search all three scopes in one go (log output; empty pattern returns capped “all” style hits) |
| `find --history <pattern>` | Search recent history titles/URLs only |
| `find --bookmark <pattern>` | Search bookmark titles/URLs only |
| `find --page <pattern>` | Search visible text in loaded http(s) tabs; may prompt for optional site access at runtime |
| `close` / `c <tabId>` | Close a tab |
| `group new <tabId> …` | Create a group |
| `notes` | Show release notes for the current extension version; `notes <version>` for a specific version (e.g. `0.1.1`); `notes --list` lists versions that have entries |
PowerSearch — Tab Search, History Search & Tab Manager
Instantly search all open tabs and browser history with one shortcut. Find any tab in seconds. Privacy-first, no login required

PowerSearch lets you find any open tab or page you've ever visited — instantly. Hit Cmd+Shift+F (or Ctrl+Shift+F on Windows) and start typing. No more cycling through dozens of tabs to find that one JIRA ticket, Docs file, or Stack Overflow answer.


INSTANT TAB SEARCH
Search across all your open tabs in real time. PowerSearch finds matches by page title, URL, or content. Click a result and jump straight to that tab — no more squinting at tiny favicon titles.

FULL HISTORY SEARCH
Go beyond open tabs. PowerSearch searches your complete browser history so you can find pages you visited days or weeks ago. Never lose a page again.

FREE FEATURES (No Account Required)
- Unlimited tab search across all open tabs
- 30 days of history search
- Keyboard shortcut activation (Cmd+Shift+F / Ctrl+Shift+F)
- Privacy-first: your data never leaves your browser

PRO FEATURES ($9.99 — One-Time Purchase, Not a Subscription)
- Unlimited history search (no 30-day limit)
- Side panel mode — keep PowerSearch open while you browse
- Pinned searches — save frequent queries for one-click access
- Domain filters — narrow results to specific sites
- Priority support

PRIVACY FIRST
PowerSearch runs 100% locally in your browser. No login required. No tracking. No data sent to any server. Your browsing data stays on your machine.

WHO IS THIS FOR?
- Developers juggling JIRA, GitHub, Stack Overflow, and docs tabs
- Researchers with dozens of reference tabs open
- Anyone who's ever lost a tab and spent 5 minutes clicking through them all
- Power users who want their browser to work like a searchable workspace

KEYBOARD SHORTCUT
Mac: Cmd+Shift+F
Windows/Linux: Ctrl+Shift+F

Suggests opening suspicious content in incognito mode

🔍 Suggest Incognito: an intelligent assistant that automatically suggests opening a tab in private mode when you visit suspicious websites. For extra privacy, the tab is instantly closed and removed from your browser history.

📝 How to enable private mode:
➤ Toolbar icon – click the extension icon to instantly open the current tab in an incognito window.
➤ Context menu – right-click on a page and choose “Open this tab in incognito.”
➤ Automatic suggestion – the extension proactively suggests switching to incognito when suspicious content is detected.

🕶️ Incognito means:
	•	Definition: keeping your activity private and anonymous.
	•	Web: incognito mode prevents saving browsing history, cookies, and site data.
	•	Suggest Incognito: goes further by adding automatic prompts and removing traces during your session.

🚀 Seamless compatibility
Suggest Incognito works across all websites: social networks, online stores, news portals. Wherever you browse, the extension guarantees instant private access.

🎨 Simple interface
The interface is intuitive and accessible. With just one click, you switch to incognito mode — no extra steps or complex settings.

👥 Who needs Suggest Incognito:
	1.	Privacy-minded users.
	2.	Professionals handling sensitive data (SEO, marketing, analytics).
	3.	Parents concerned about family digital safety.
	4.	Web developers and testers who need clean sessions.
	5.	Everyday users who want quick access to private browsing.

🌈 Flexibility and functionality
You can customize behavior: automatically close original tabs, erase history completely, or keep only private windows.

🌐 Incognito in a nutshell
Suggest Incognito makes privacy effortless: it suggests switching automatically, erases history instantly, and ensures a clean digital footprint.

🔐 Maximum privacy
▸ Automatic prompts to switch on suspicious websites.
▸ History removal for complete erasure of browsing traces.
▸ Close the original tab and open a private copy — for full control and security.

📈 User satisfaction
Early testers highlight the perfect balance between simplicity and power: smart prompts, flexible settings, and confidence in privacy.

🔒 Conclusion
Suggest Incognito is a smart Chrome extension that takes privacy to the next level. With automatic suggestions, history cleanup, and a user-friendly interface, it’s a must-have tool for anyone who values security and seamless private browsing.

A new patented shortcut tool that calls history and bookmarks from 'just the right spot'. Also restores tabs and calls them in bulk.

Ver.3.1.5 In addition to the traditional mode, we've added a mode where the bookmark list opens simply by hovering over the ghost interface. We've also implemented light mode and dark mode switching. Click the extension icon to open the settings screen and configure to your preference.

Source code:
https://github.com/ogiharadevelopment/GhostInTheChrome

The red dashed line does not appear on the browser's top page. Please use it while actually browsing the web.

Video 1: A video demonstrating the user experience.

Image 1: Point to the red dashed line with your mouse.

Image 2: Rotate the wheel between the mouse buttons. Press ”;” to open detailed instructions.

Image 3: Scroll up to open history, scroll down to open bookmarks. For long lists, you can open from just the right position.


Transparent Five-Tool Gadget
１.Restore tabs
２.Open specified pages all at once
３.Jump to the perfect point in your history
４.Jump to the perfect point in your bookmarks
５.Quick access to your favorite pages

Just hover the mouse over the Ghost Interface—outlined in red at the upper right of any page—and press keyboard keys or scroll the wheel. That single action switches among five features.
The Ghost Interface allows clicks to pass through.

1. Save every open tab as URLs for later.
The Ghost Interface has three zones: left, center, and right. Each zone changes the red outline to yellow, green, or blue when you hover over it.
Yellow (left) + number key (1–9): stores the URLs of all open tabs under that number.
Green (center) + number key: shows the list tied to that number.
Blue (right) + number key: opens every page from that list at once.

2. Save the entire session and restore it with a single key.
This uses Chrome’s native session restore feature. Operation is the same as above, but you use letter keys (a–z) instead of numbers.

3. View bookmark lists from exactly the right spot.
Hover inside the interface to display “N%.” N represents the pointer’s position between the left edge (0%) and right edge (100%). If you have 100 bookmarks, moving to 50% and scrolling down shows the list starting around the 50th bookmark.

4. View browsing history from exactly the right spot.
With N% visible, scrolling up reveals history using the same logic. Pressing Ctrl inside the interface cycles through modes: normal → border hidden → sleep → back to normal.
Pressing @ while hovering in the center displays a list of recently closed tabs/windows at the corresponding N% position.

5. Manage your personal “GhostFAB” favorites.
Shift + wheel up adds the current page to GhostFAB.
Shift + wheel down shows the GhostFAB list.

This extension features the Ghost Interface GUI, already granted a patent by the Japan Patent Office. Additional related patents are pending.
